2606.18056.title: ConSA: Controllable Sparsity in Hybrid Attention via Learnable Allocation
2606.18056.paper_summary: Hybrid architectures combining full attention (FA) and sliding-window attention (SWA) are a promising paradigm for efficient LLM inference. However, existing methods typically rely on hand-crafted rules or simple post-hoc heuristics for FA/SWA allocation and offer limited analysis of the attention behaviors underlying these designs. We propose Controllable Sparsity in Hybrid Attention (ConSA), a framework that learns optimal FA/SWA assignment under a user-specified sparsity target. ConSA employs L0 regularization to learn binary masks selecting between FA and SWA for each attention unit, while an augmented Lagrangian constraint enforces the target sparsity at either layer or KV-head granularity. We evaluate ConSA on two LLMs at the 0.6B and 1.7B scales. Learned allocations consistently outperform rule-based baselines, with KV-head-wise allocation yielding clear gains over layer-wise allocation. The learned patterns place SWA in the bottom layers and concentrate FA into contiguous middle-layer blocks, diverging from evenly interleaved patterns in rule-based methods. This structure persists across model scales, sparsity levels, and allocation granularities, revealing a fine-grained spectrum of intrinsic attention behaviors that underlies the learned allocation.
2606.18056.paper_summary_zh: 混合架構結合了全注意力（FA）和滑動窗口注意力（SWA），是一種有效的 LLM 推理的有希望的範式。<lb/>然而，現有的方法通常依賴手工製作的規則或簡單的事後啟發式方法來進行 FA/SWA 分配，並且對這些設計背後的注意力行為提供有限的分析。<lb/>我們提出了可控稀疏性的混合注意力（ConSA），這是一個在用戶指定的稀疏目標下學習最佳 FA/SWA 分配的框架。<lb/>ConSA 使用 L0 正則化來學習二進制掩碼，為每個注意力單元選擇 FA 和 SWA，而增強拉格朗日約束則在層級或 KV 頭粒度上強制執行目標稀疏性。<lb/>我們在兩個 LLM（0.6B 和 1.7B 規模）上評估了 ConSA。<lb/>學習的分配始終超越基於規則的基準，其中 KV 頭級的分配相對於層級分配帶來了明顯的增益。<lb/>學習的模式將 SWA 放置在底層，並將 FA 集中在連續的中層區塊中，與基於規則的方法中均勻交錯的模式相背離。<lb/>這種結構在模型規模、稀疏水平和分配粒度上持續存在，揭示了潛在的注意力行為的細緻光譜，這些行為是學習分配的基礎。
2606.18056.author: Yao Chen et.al.
2606.18056.authors: Yao Chen, Yinqi Yang, Junyuan Shang, Xiangzhao Hao, Simeng Zhang, Yilong Chen, Tingwen Liu, Shuohuan Wang, Dianhai Yu
